content: Just as the pedagogical definition of reflexion as a core learning vocabulary in high school English frames repeated intentional reflection on language use as the way to internalize implicit linguistic intuition, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to adopt a spaced, vocabulary-style repetition framework to accumulate granular self-critique knowledge instead of only performing ad-hoc reflection on single task outputs. This turns the cognitive practice of human reflective language learning into a blueprint for improving autonomous LLM self-reflection.
